Chapter One

The Writing System

Liaserok — The Cythora Alphabet

Cythora is a constructed language with its own script, phonology, and grammar. Its writing system is built around a principle of spatial composition: each written unit (called a chunk) is a visual cluster made of one central base letter and up to three surrounding sound modifiers. The aesthetic concept is "satellites orbiting a planet" — a large anchor glyph at the center, with smaller modifier glyphs positioned precisely around it.

H-Rule

There is no base letter for H. No word or chunk may begin with h. If an English source word starts with h, drop it — Harry → Arry, Harsh → Arsh. H exists only as a floating sound symbol.

Liaserok The 18 Base Letters
A
B
D
E
F
G
I / Y
J / Z
K / Q / X
L
M
N
O / U
P
R
S / C
T
V / W

Letter Equivalences

CythoraEnglishCythoraEnglishCythoraEnglish
AAI / YI and Y share one glyphO / UO and U share one glyph
BBJ / ZJ and Z share one glyphPP
DDK / Q / XK, Q, X share one glyphRR
EELLS / CS and C share one glyph
FFMMTT
GGNNV / WV and W share one glyph

Substitution Rules

Apply these before writing any English word in Cythora script:

ReplaceWithExampleReplaceWithExample
CScircle → sirsleZJzenith → jenith
YIcity → sitiUOsun → son
WVwater → vaterX, QKwax → vak

Chunk Types & Writing Styles

StyleChars encodedStructure
1-base-1-sound2Sound floats above the base letter
1-base-2-sounds3One sound left, one right of base
1-base-3-sounds4One top, one left, one right of base

Chunking Priority

Always prefer the longest chunk: 4-char → 3-char → 2-char. No word should end with a lone base letter. Quick guide: 5 letters = 3+2  ·  6 = 4+2  ·  7 = 4+3  ·  8 = 4+4  ·  9 = 4+3+2  ·  10 = 4+4+2  ·  12 = 4+4+4

Chapter Two

The 19 Sound Symbols

Ohom — Floating marks that attach to base letters

Cythora has 19 sound symbols — one more than the 18 base letters. The extra one is H, which exists only as a sound mark and has no base letter. These symbols float above, left, or right of a base letter to form a chunk.

Ohom The 19 Sound Symbols
A
B
D
E
F
G
H
I / Y
J / Z
K / Q / X
L
M
N
O / U
P
R
S / C
T
V / W

H as Sound Only

H has no base letter form, so it can never begin a chunk. It can appear as a floating mark in the middle or end position of any chunk — this is how words like khed, ohkodra, and klodambelsik are written.

Chapter Three

Punctuation

Kidhava — Marks, Enclosers & Pairs

Unlike English, most Cythora punctuation marks are enclosing pairs — the sentence is wrapped between a mirrored open and close symbol. Only the comma functions like its English equivalent.

Kidhava Punctuation Marks
· · ·
Question Mark

Symmetric enclosing pair — the sentence sits between both halves.

· · ·
Full Stop

Envelope mark — the sentence is enclosed between two matching symbols.

· · ·
Exclamation Mark

Enclosing pair — wraps exclamatory sentences and commands.

· · ·
Quotation Marks

Symmetric pair — used for both single and double quotes.

Comma

Behaves identically to the English comma — no enclosing function.

Does Not Exist

Cythora has no: colon · semi-colon · dash · hyphen · apostrophe · parentheses () [] {{}} · ellipsis ...

Chapter Four

Core Vocabulary

Greetings, Pronouns, Connectors & More

Greetings

Cythora has no equivalents for hello, hi, good morning, or goodnight. Instead there are 14 clan-based greetings, all equal in weight and formality. Any may be used at any time. All follow the pattern synth ra [spirit-word] — invoking the spirit of a body part or quality.

  • synth ra kalibSpirit of wingsGakirakh
  • synth ra tinkivSpirit of holdEmborakh
  • synth ra kredejSpirit of clawsKimbrakh
  • synth ra batnidSpirit of actionsElrakh
  • synth ra borakSpirit of headRadhrakh
  • synth ra OsovoSpirit of breathTathorakh
  • synth ra vajovaSpirit of leapArakh
  • synth ra bekonshSpirit of tailOlrakh
  • synth ra nikemSpirit of eyesBabirakh
  • synth ra OsitoSpirit of mindVabirakh
  • synth ra OkelomeSpirit of earsKinrakh
  • synth ra OkotegSpirit of bodyVelrakh
  • synth ra dorikedSpirit of scalesSimorakh
  • synth ra indosoSpirit of heartInorakh

Agreement, Apology & Thanks

EnglishCythoraNotes
Yes / I acceptlobavikFormal: oiva lobavikdo
No / NotkhedAlso the universal negation marker
I'm sorrykragoteAdd object pronoun: kragote vasi = sorry for you
It's okayoteaNegative: khotea
Thank youbelondrat oivam indosoLit. "you have my heart" · informal: vabelovin
You're welcometovis nan dab vimitagLit. "until and after death" · informal: kandav

Chapter Five

Grammar Fundamentals

Structure, Questions, Negation & Special Logic

FeatureRule
Word orderSVO — Subject, Verb, Object (same as English)
ArticlesNone. No a/an/the. All nouns are general — singular and plural are identical.
Verb infinitiveAlways ends in -dra: klodra = to walk, rakdra = to do, andra = to be
Negationkhed after the object in simple sentences; after the helping-verb clause when a main verb follows
QuestionsSame word order as statements; interrogative word moves to front; enclosed in question mark symbols
Question tagsAll question tags → orak
Requestsosorashi at end of any request sentence, replacing "please"

Questions & Negation

?Onak vat rakdam?What are you doing?
?Andrat ajkifova? / ?Andrat ajkifova khed?Are you late? / Aren't you late?
?Demdrat iso, orak?You want it, don't you?
Demdo orov khedI don't want that. (khed after the object)
Demdo khed klodraI don't want to walk. (khed before the infinitive)
sadamad osi osorashiPlease help me.
!Dojavd! → !Dojavd khed!Go! → Don't go!

There Is / There Are

EnglishCythoraEnglishCythora
There isVovi androThere areVovi andaj
There was / wereVovi andreThis is / That isirov andro / orov andro
These are / Those arejirov andaj / jorov andaj

Relative Clauses

Almost all relative clause connectors (who, which, that, whose) translate to the connector ojao.

Indo ojao jaldat ovi andat doktor.The woman who lives here is a doctor.
Ret ohkodre osi ojao ret dojavrako.He told me that he would go.

Verb Prefixes

EnglishPrefixExampleEnglishPrefixExample
out-oso-outdo → osorakdraover-oji-overdo → ojirakdra
mis-sor-misjudge → soromadvasadraun-ki-undo → kirakdra
re-iro-redo → irorakdrapre-ori-preplan → orisedorofdra
under-ano-underestimate → anoteskesedradis-kio-disappear → kioropabidra

Imperatives

Remove -ra from -dra. The verb stem + d is the command form.

rakd orovDo that!
snard osi losd isoWatch me throw it.

Chapter Six

The Verb System

Batnio — Conjugations, All Tenses & Modals

Batnio Verb

All infinitives end in -dra. To conjugate: remove -dra, apply the suffix below. The example verb throughout is klodra (to walk). All suffixes are the same for every subject unless noted.

Simple Present — Subject-Specific Endings

SubjectEndingklodra →SubjectEndingklodra →
oiva (I)-doklodojet (We)-dejklodej
vat (You)-dratklodratjat (They)-dajklodaj
ret (He)-detklodetova (It)-droklodro
rat (She)-datklodat

All Tenses — Suffix Reference

TenseSuffixExample (klodra)
PRESENT
Present Continuous-damklodam — I am walking
Present Perfect-tbelklotbel — I have walked
Present Perfect Continuous-dambelklodambel — I have been walking
PAST
Simple Past-dreklodre — I walked
Past Continuous-damaniklodamani — I was walking
Past Perfect-tbeloklotbelo — I had walked
Past Perfect Continuous-dambeloklodambelo — I had been walking
FUTURE
Simple Future (will/going to) — subject-conjugated + -rakconj+-rakklodorak (I) · klodratrak (you)…
Was going to / was about to — subject-conjugated + -rakeconj+-rakeklodorake (I) · klodratrake (you)…
Future Continuous-damrakiklodamraki — I will be walking
Future Perfect-tanikklotanik — I will have walked
Future Perfect Continuous-damanikklodamanik — I will have been walking
MODALS
Can / Able to-kiokklokiok — I can walk
Could · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-sik -damsik -belsik -dambelsikklosik / klodamsik / klobelsik…
Would · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-rako -damrako -belrako -berakklorako / klodamrako…
Should · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-dok -damok -belok -damkoklodok / klodamok…
Shall · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-doko -damoko -toko -belokoklodoko / klodamoko…
Must · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-mo -damo -belmo -dambelmoklomo / klodamo…
May · Continuous · Perfect · Perf. Cont.-dka -damka -tka -dambelkaklodka / klodamka…
Might — subject-conjugated + -kaconj+-kaklodoka (I) · klodratka (you) · klodetka (he)…
HAVE TO / GOTTA
Have to · Had to · Will have to-dig -dik -dijklodig / klodik / klodij
Would · Could · Shall have to-dis -did -difklodis / klodid / klodif
Should · May · Might have to-dih -dil -dimklodih / klodil / klodim

Might — Special Rule

Might is the only modal that is subject-conjugated. Apply the simple present endings first, then add -ka: oiva → klodoka, vat → klodratka, ret → klodetka, rat → klodatka, jet → klodejka, jat → klodajka, ova → klodroka.

Sample Sentences

oiva demdo khed lobshodraI don't want to play.
ratslo demd bidra vasiShe just wants to see you.
oiva nikigadamve khed isoI didn't keep tracking it.
jat lobavikamoj khed okad jasiThey are not accepted by them.
ova athomoj vitimagdraIt's said to die.

Chapter Seven

The Adjective Mechanism

Mikelpena — Degrees, Polarity & the 45 Root Axes

Mikelpena Adjective

Cythora adjectives are built systematically from three components:

Degree
mandatory
+
ki · polarity
optional
+
Root Axis
mandatory

Degree Markers

MarkerDegreeMeaningMarkerDegreeMeaning
oi-Minimalbarely, trace ofag-Lowsomewhat, mildly
as-Neutralmoderately, standardaj-Highnoticeably, quite
kli-Extremeextremely, utterly

Polarity (ki): inserted between degree and root, it reverses or negates the axis. For color roots, it means desaturated/faded. Example: ajgobpol (strong) → ajkigobpol (weak).

Selected Confirmed Adjectives

CythoraEnglishCythoraEnglishCythoraEnglish
ajbovasbig/largeajkimilebadajnotmesafe
ajhafbiklongajgobpolstrongajkinotmedangerous
ajkihafbikshort (length)kligobpolpowerfulajdresilbrave/free
ajapstatall/highajkigobpolweakajfordimclose/near
ajkiapstadeep/short (height)ajvikavbeautifulajkifordimfar
ajpraniswideagvikavsweetajmioloud
ajkipranisnarrowajkivikavbitter/uglyajkimioquiet
ajfovaquick/fast/earlyajgobodhonest/clearklikimiosilent
ajkifovaslow/lateajpikoddifficultajliokind
klifovaswift/immediateajkipikodsimple/easyagliogentle
ajmilegoodklipikodlaboriousajteskold/aged
asmilefineajerbegright/certainkliteskancient
ajikahsharpajkisromokstrange/differentajsenforclean/pure
ajpretohard (material)ajmorfsmoothajkisenfordirty
ajkipretosoft (feel)ajkimorfrough/coarseasioround/circular
ajelekshotajbrofopheavyajkiioangular/pointed
agelekswarmajinflacomplete/fullklierbegwise
ajkielekscoldoiinflabare/minimaloiflokrare

Degrees of Comparison

FormRuleExample
PositiveBase adjectiveajbovas = big
ComparativeAdd prefix oni-oniajbovas = bigger · onikiletodam = more exciting
SuperlativeAdd suffix -oniajbovasoni = biggest · kiletodamoni = most exciting

Adjectives from Verbs

TypeRuleExample
Present participle (exciting, running)Verb stem + -damkiletodra → kiletodam = exciting
Past participle passive (excited, wanted)Verb stem + passive suffix for the tenseI am excited → oiva kiletomoj · I was wanted → oiva demroj

Adverbs

All derived adverbs: append -nok to the adjective form. klibovas (massive) → klibovasnok (massively). Stacking order for multiple adverbs/adjectives: manner/speed/physical → control/difficulty → evaluative/abstract.

Chapter Eight

Passive Voice

All Forms — Standard, Infinitive & Active Infinitive

In the passive, the original object becomes the subject and the original subject becomes the agent (follows the verb). The active tense suffix is replaced by the corresponding passive suffix. I tell them → They are told by me.

Passive Suffix Reference

TensePassive SuffixTensePassive Suffix
STANDARD PASSIVE
Simple Present-mojSimple Past-roj
Present Continuous-madPast Continuous-rad
Present Perfect-motPast Perfect-rot
Simple Future-kojFuture Perfect-kaj
MODAL PASSIVE
Would · Continuous · Perfect-aoj -aad -aotCan be-koik
Could · Continuous · Perfect-soj -sad -sotShould · Continuous · Perfect-doj -dad -dot
Shall · Continuous · Perfect-okoj -okad -okotMay · Continuous · Perfect-ojka -adka -otka
Might · Continuous · Perfect-tioj -tiad -tiotMust · Continuous · Perfect-jom -ojad -jot
INFINITIVE PASSIVE
Passive Infinitive (to be done)-mogContinuous Passive Infinitive (to be being done)-sog
Passive Perfect Infinitive (to have been done)-logActive Continuous Infinitive (to be doing)-dam — treat as continuous tense
Active Perfect Infinitive (to have done)-tbel — treat as perfect tenseActive Perf. Cont. Infinitive-dambel — treat as perf. cont. tense
jet kashavdej dermokobmojWe need to be separated.
ova rokoseroj litsilogIt was expected to have been finished.
jat oamsimiroj rokvadamThey are believed to be working.
Jat rokoseroj litsitbelThey are expected to have finished.

Chapter Nine

Special Constructions

Keep, Used To, Just & Emphasis

Keep + Verb-ing

EnglishSuffixEnglishSuffix
keep + -ing-damvohave kept + -ing-belvo
kept + -ing-damvehad kept + -ing-belve
will keep + -ing-damviwill have kept + -ing-belvi
would keep + -ing-damiwould have kept + -ing-beli
can keep + -ing-kiokocould keep + -ing-siko
could have kept + -ing-sikeshall keep + -ing-dokoi
shall have kept + -ing-dokoeshould keep + -ing-doki
should have kept + -ing-dokemay keep + -ing-dkai
may have kept + -ing-dkaemight keep + -ing-kai
might have kept + -ing-kaemust keep + -ing-moi
must have kept + -ing-moehave to keep + -ing-digi
had to keep + -ing-dikiwill have to keep + -ing-diji
oiva kisosindamvoI keep forgetting.
ratslo soboshdamvoShe just keeps talking.

Used To Constructions

EnglishSuffixEnglishSuffix
used to-jekget used to-jeka
am/are getting used to-damjahave gotten used to-belja
have been getting used to-dabjagot used to-jekb
had gotten used to-beljbwill get used to-jeks
would get used to-jekdcan get used to-jeke
could get used to-jekfshall get used to-jekg
should get used to-jekhmay get used to-jeki
might get used to-jekkmust get used to-jekl
have to get used to-kejhad to get used to-kek

Keep Getting Used To

EnglishSuffixEnglishSuffix
keep getting used to-fahave kept getting used to-fe
kept getting used to-fihad kept getting used to-fo
will keep getting used to-foowould keep getting used to-fea
can keep getting used to-feocould keep getting used to-feoo
shall keep getting used to-faishould keep getting used to-faoo
may keep getting used to-fiemight keep getting used to-fioo
must keep getting used to-foehave to keep getting used to-sa

Just + Verb

FormSubject modificationVerb formExample
Just + presentAppend slo to subjectVerb stem + d (drop -ra)ratslo demd = She just wants
Just + pastAppend go to subjectSame as aboveretgo ohkod = He just told
ratslo demd bidra vasiShe just wants to see you.
retgo ohkod vasi orovHe just told you that.

Do/Did Emphasis

Emphatic structures like "I do believe you" or "I did know that" are treated as "I indeed believe you" — use the adverb anfja (indeed) or anfva (in fact) and translate the rest normally. The emphasis is conveyed through context and adverb choice.

Chapter Ten

The Fourteen Clans

Shingo — Emblems, Script Names & Slogans

Shingo Clan

Cythora is the shared language of fourteen clans. Their names form the only greetings in the language, their colour systems carry cultural identity, and their slogans are the closest thing Cythora has to proverbs. Each entry shows the clan's name in the Cythorian script alongside their circular emblem.

Cythora — in the Cythorian script
Cythorakh — the people of Cythora

Simorakh

The Echoes of Justice

Greeting: synth ra doriked

Tomisog andro okan ajgobpoloni arogekdro nan ajkigobpoloni barvitamoj.

"Power is when the strongest falls and the weak is influenced."

Vabirakh

The Interplanetary Explorers and Learners

Greeting: synth ra Osito

Osidovismoj osanjosi kherodro keravar tok obevo.

"An educated system always asks for justice."

Tathorakh

The Eternal Compensators

Greeting: synth ra Osovo

Ojaropd keko dof oamsimid khratiki gonen.

"Worship the dust but believe in the creaks more."

Gakirakh

The Legendary Gliders

Greeting: synth ra kalib

Gevag miragedro egapo obak kiriokasao.

"Speed disguises progress with stagnancy."

Babirakh

The Comforters of the Lost

Greeting: synth ra nikem

Gored ovadej andaj vodima kisosindej.

"The monsters we become are the values we forget."

Elrakh

The Universal Fabricators

Greeting: synth ra batnid

Daravd sinth vasno nan kamanodrorak ovam noviko simo bek.

"Give a soul awareness and it'll write its doom first."

Radhrakh

The Cosmic Advisors

Greeting: synth ra borak

Virosok andro obsta ojegis mosarok kashavdro kostrokoldra siobid.

"Revelation is the only uplifting one needs to gain perspective."

Emborakh

The Keepers of Corpses and Reminders

Greeting: synth ra tinkiv

Khed kegon gored andro krekdok.

"Not every monster is a sinner."

Velrakh

The Generous Producers

Greeting: synth ra Okoteg

Valomsit nan jalhako vagon andaj vabi bek kragj.

"Resurrection and Survival are both second chances."

Olrakh

The Ancient Sorcerers

Greeting: synth ra bekonsh

Evelokdka rikovar kamanomoj nan orosokmoj.

"May the written and the happened never meet."

Kimbrakh

The Strategic Offenders

Greeting: synth ra kredej

Adrek belondaj nikem ojao nisinrokdaj vatam rako vais avimel.

"The shadows have eyes which reflect your deeds without light."

Kinrakh

The Interdisciplinary Inquisitors

Greeting: synth ra Okelome

Sitotikmoj osanjosi kherodro keravar tok vivaro.

"An evolved system always asks for the truth."

Arakh

The Strategic Defenders

Greeting: synth ra vajova

Seratomakh tohokdro simo bek vinkoshmoj keravar ra ovam adrek.

"The one who strikes first is always afraid of its own shadow."

Inorakh

The Interplanetary Mediators

Greeting: synth ra indoso

Kragj daravdro khed vasi avisob soprojedra.

"Opportunity doesn't give you the right to win."

✦ synth ra indoso ✦
